author: Ameen Hassan
summary: This session was a queue operation invoking the session memory extractor on a prior conversation transcript. The input transcript was itself a queue operation that wrote a canonical project summary for the ideas-lab project. The prior session's output described ideas-lab as the IDeaS Revenue Solutions internship project, characterizing the work as AI-driven solutions for hospitality and revenue management. The memory extractor was applied to produce a structured JSON memory object from that canonical summary session. No new project work was performed; the session's sole function was structured memory extraction from a prior automated operation.
problems: No canonical project summary existed for ideas-lab prior to the prior session
The prior session noted that the IDeaS_Workspace directory was not found during an automated weekly sync
approaches: Queue-operation pattern used to extract structured memory from a transcript that was itself a queue operation
Session memory extractor applied recursively to a canonical summary generation session
results: Valid JSON memory object produced for the ideas-lab canonical summary session
Structured memory captured: ideas-lab characterized as IDeaS Revenue Solutions internship work on AI-driven hospitality/revenue management solutions

decisions.decision: Treat recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role

open_questions: Was the canonical summary for ideas-lab actually persisted to the MCP server after the prior session?
Why was the IDeaS_Workspace directory not found during the automated weekly sync?
